tagged_story_for_tts:
Now I will teach you critical_thinking:<[silence:80ms]>

When you see tracks in mud, you do not say "big beast!" just because tracks are big—you check:<[silence:80ms]> deep or shallow?<[silence:110ms]> Fresh or old?<[silence:110ms]> Claws or hooves?<[silence:110ms]> You smell air, look for broken branches, listen for sounds.<[silence:60ms]> Only then do you know "lion" or "just buffalo." Critical_thinking is holding your spear back until your eyes, nose, and hands agree on what is true—not guessing, not fearing, not wanting.<[silence:60ms]> Do this with words, with fire, with tribe talk.<[silence:60ms]> Wrong guess = dead caveman.<[silence:60ms]> Right thinking = live caveman.<[silence:60ms]>
clean_story:
Now I will teach you critical_thinking:

When you see tracks in mud, you do not say "big beast!" just because tracks are big—you check: deep or shallow? Fresh or old? Claws or hooves? You smell air, look for broken branches, listen for sounds. Only then do you know "lion" or "just buffalo." Critical_thinking is holding your spear back until your eyes, nose, and hands agree on what is true—not guessing, not fearing, not wanting. Do this with words, with fire, with tribe talk. Wrong guess = dead caveman. Right thinking = live caveman.
